category_title: Fourth-grade students who initially dislike reading but engage through self-selection
student_description: This student initially exhibits strong task resistance toward reading, often claiming to hate it when forced to use standardized basal readers, and tends to read superficially just to finish. They uniquely require autonomy through self-selected texts and open-ended, conversational prompts that connect reading to their personal lives to transition from resistance to deep, genuine engagement.

criteria.rationale: Asking open-ended questions about text features draws this superficial reader into a genuine conversation without triggering their resistance to specific-answer solicitation.
criteria.check: The tutor asks an open-ended question about what the student notices regarding the text's features (e.g., characters, genre) rather than fishing for a specific predetermined answer.
